pattern: Only adjacent-pair sums matter, so a single pass that records each seen pair sum in a set detects a repeat the moment it occurs.
hints: Every candidate subarray has length two, so there are only n-1 sums to consider.
As you slide a window of size two across the array, you just need to know whether a sum has appeared before.
Add consecutive pairs' sums to a set while scanning; if a sum is already present, two distinct pairs share it, so return true.

edge_inputs.description: identical adjacent pairs from repeated zeros
minimum length, single pair, no repeat possible
near-collision that does not actually match

stress.description: random arrays over a narrow value range to force frequent collisions
